raw_text:
Sarah: Thanks for joining today. Can you share more about your current infrastructure challenges?

James: Sure. We're running about 450 users right now on a legacy system. We're looking to migrate by Q1 next year — probably scaling to around 500 users once the new hires come in. The Enterprise subscription looks like the right fit.

Priya: From a finance perspective, we have budget approved. We'd need pricing details before we can present internally. Also, our security team will want to see your security documentation before anything moves forward.

Sarah: Absolutely. I'll send over the security questionnaire today and follow up with pricing this week.

James: One more thing — you may still have us listed as Velora in your system. We rebranded to Etihad Airport Services earlier this year. Please update your records.

Sarah: Got it, will do. Is Q1 a hard deadline?

Priya: Yes, April 1st at the latest. Missing that window pushes us to Q3 due to budget cycles.

Laura: We've been evaluating three vendors and honestly your demo last week was the strongest. We're looking at roughly 200 seats to start.

Marcus: Great to hear. What's the timeline looking like?

Laura: We want to close by end of Q2. Budget is around $80,000 for the first year. That said, we'll need to see a formal proposal before legal gets involved.

Marcus: I'll get that over to you by end of week. Any concerns we should address?

Laura: The main one is data residency — we need everything in EU region. That's a hard requirement.

Marcus: Understood, I'll flag that for our infrastructure team and confirm in the proposal.

participants: Priya Nair (VP Finance, Etihad Airport Services)
raw_text:
Sarah: Hi James, Priya — following up from our last call. I sent over the security questionnaire last week. Did your team get a chance to review it?

James: Yes, we reviewed it internally. Everything looks good. Security team signed off.

Priya: We also reviewed the pricing. For 500 users on Enterprise, we'd be looking at the $375,000 annual figure you quoted. That fits within our approved budget.

Sarah: Perfect. I can now send over the formal proposal. Shall I address it to Etihad Airport Services?

James: Yes, that's correct. Etihad Airport Services is the legal entity. Make sure the contract header reflects that.

Priya: The April 1st deadline still holds. We need the signed contract by then to activate before Q2 budget cycle closes.

Sarah: Understood. I'll have the proposal to you by end of week.

Tom: Look, I'll be direct. We went with a competitor last week. The pricing was significantly lower and they had better native integrations with our existing stack.

Diana: I appreciate you telling me directly. Was there anything specific that swayed the decision?

Tom: The integration story was the biggest factor. Your team couldn't commit to a native Salesforce connector timeline.

Diana: That's fair feedback. Is there any chance of revisiting if the integration gap closes?

Tom: Possibly in 12 months during the next renewal cycle. We'd consider a re-evaluation then.
